title: If SpaceX’s Moonshots Fail, It Will Always Have Starlink
summary: A simple view of SpaceX is that it’s a low-cost rocket launcher that created the profitable Starlink satellite business and which is now burning cash to build orbital data centers and colonize Mars. Starlink doesn’t have the lead role in SpaceX’s $1.8 trillion initial public offering. But it is the real business inside the company, and the telecoms industry and its regulators can ill afford to be complacent about the disruptive threat it poses.

title: Volvo Eyes $3 Billion Autonomous Truck Business in Five Years
summary: Volvo AB expects revenue from autonomous transportation to approach $3 billion within five years after starting driverless operations on US highways in a few months.

title: Carlyle Seeks Banks for India IPO of Healthcare RCM Provider
summary: Carlyle Group Inc. has invited pitches from investment banks to advise on a potential India initial public offering of its recently acquired healthcare billing service business, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Cannabis Medicine for Back Pain Aims to Displace Opioids
summary: A cannabis drug for lower back pain will soon go on sale in Germany and Austria in what its developer views as a dry run for launching in the world’s biggest pharma market: the US.

summary: El escrutinio regulatorio, las acusaciones de espionaje y las denuncias de competencia desleal están marcando el tono de lo que antes era solo una batalla por la cuota de mercado.

title: Chinese Delivery Apps Are Taking Off in Brazil as Rivals Pile On Perks
summary: Regulatory scrutiny, alleged espionage and unfair competition claims are setting the tone for what was once just a battle for market share.

summary: The manager of the largest leveraged exchange-traded fund tracking SK Hynix Inc. pushed back against claims that the $10 billion product is adding to volatility in the South Korean chipmaker’s shares.

title: Workspace Slashes Values as Weak London Demand Hits Rents
summary: Workspace Group Plc, a London landlord providing flexible office space to the city’s small- and medium sized-business, reported a worse than expected loss in the year through March as falling rents and rising vacancy hit valuations.

title: Bizlink to Buy Blackstone’s Interplex Unit in $850 Million Deal
summary: Bizlink Holding Inc. has agreed to buy the information and communications technology unit of Blackstone Inc.’s Interplex in a deal valued at about $850 million.

title: Meta Partners With Reliance on First India AI Data Center
summary: Meta Platforms Inc. is partnering with Reliance Industries Ltd. to build its first AI data center in India, adding to a wave of investment in tech infrastructure globally.

title: France’s Proparco Plans Sri Lanka Investments After Default
summary: Proparco, the private-sector financing arm of Agence Française de Développement, plans to fund Sri Lankan companies involved in renewable energy, agribusiness and women-led small enterprises, according to Vincent Vandenbussche, acting regional director for South Asia.

title: Ambani-Backed Robot Startup Seeks $100 Million to Lead in India
summary: Indian startup Addverb Technologies Ltd. is seeking to raise more than $100 million, trying to cement its role as the country’s top maker of robots.

title: Governments Sell Bonds at Record Pace as Spending Soars
summary: Governments are borrowing from syndicated bond markets at a record clip as public spending surges.

title: China Taps Commercial Oil Stocks to Help Weather Gulf Shock
summary: China has started tapping its commercial crude reserves to help offset the supply shock from the Iran war, although the world’s biggest oil importer is continuing to prioritize lower refinery use and fuel export limits to manage the fallout.

title: US, Iran Exchange Strikes, Putting Lasting Peace Deal at Risk
summary: The US and Iran exchanged strikes overnight, jolting their two-month-old truce and endangering talks aimed at securing lasting peace in the Middle East.

title: Tech Selloff Deepens as Traders Brace for Hot CPI: Markets Wrap
summary: Technology stocks were set to pull US equities lower for a second straight session as traders prepared for an inflation report that is expected to show the highest reading in more than three years.

title: Oil Erases Gain as Traders Weigh Fallout From US-Iran Strikes
summary: Oil erased gains as traders weighed the outlook for peace talks over the Iran war after a brief US military campaign against the Islamic Republic following the downing of an American helicopter.

title: Dubai’s DP World Meets Bond Investors to Allay Worries Over War
summary: DP World Ltd., one of the world’s biggest container port operators, has been meeting with fixed-income investors to discuss its performance since the outbreak of the US-Iran war as the Dubai logistics giant mulls its options for repaying a bond due in a few months.

title: Bitcoin Finds a Bounce, Not a Bottom
summary: The cryptocurrency’s modest rebound may prove short-lived as structural frailties are exposed.

title: Gold Slumps Below $4,200 as Renewed US-Iran Clashes Test Truce
summary: Gold extended a decline after the US and Iran exchanged strikes following the downing of an American military helicopter, jeopardizing efforts to end the war that’s roiled global markets and raised inflation risks.

title: JPMorgan-Led Group to Sell Qualtrics Hung Debt at Year End
summary: A group of banks led by JPMorgan Chase & Co. is planning to offload about $5.3 billion in financing for software firm Qualtrics International Inc. later this year in a bid to shift the hung debt off their books.

title: Wedding Inflation Has Brides Paying Witches For Perfect Days
summary: Picture this: It’s the week of your wedding. You’re staring down a forecast of clouds and rain. What do you do? A lot of brides have taken to hiring a weather witch. Josyana Joshua explains. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Anthropic Releases New Mythos-Like Model Ahead of IPO | The Opening Trade 6/10/2026
summary:
Anthropic has released its latest Mythos model, Fable 5, though with one major caveat. The AI will reportedly be blocked from responding to queries related to cybersecurity and biology. It comes as the company races to IPO, along with rivals SpaceX and OpenAI.

The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Anna Edwards and Tom Mackenzie. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oracle Earnings Pose Next Test for Suddenly Shaky AI Stock Rally
summary: The rally in artificial intelligence-related stocks is suddenly on shaky footing, and Oracle Corp.’s earnings report after the close represents the group’s next test.

title: Xinjiang Chief Touts Trade, Diplomacy as Hormuz Disruption Bites
summary: China is pitching its far-western Xinjiang region as a commercial gateway to Central Asia and Europe, courting neighbors at a moment when overland trade routes are drawing renewed attention due to shipping disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Slowing Economy Gives Turkey Cover to Hold Interest Rates Again
summary: Turkey’s central bank is poised to leave its benchmark interest rate unchanged in the face of a cooling economy and subdued foreign currency demand despite rising prices driven by the Iran war.

title: South Africa’s Eskom Spinoff Plan Raises Credit Concerns
summary: South Africa’s plan to split power utility Eskom Holdings SOC Ltd. into separate units could weaken the state-owned company’s risk profile because the transmission assets account for a major share of its earnings, according to a bondholder and a ratings agency.

title: WH Smith Cuts Outlook, Seeks Capital as Iran War Hurts Sales
summary: WH Smith Plc lowered its profit outlook and set out a plan to raise capital as the fallout from the war in the Middle East weighs on sales at its network of airport stores.

title: Suez Canal Gets Oil-Tanker Boost Amid Hormuz Strait Shutdown
summary: The number of oil tankers crossing Egypt’s Suez Canal surged by almost a third in April and drove revenue to the highest since early 2024, as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz spurred an alternative Red Sea energy route.

title: Malaysia Palm Reserves Jump Most in Five Months as Exports Drop
summary: Palm oil stockpiles in Malaysia climbed at the fastest pace in five months as a sharp drop in exports overshadowed weaker production in the world’s second-largest grower.

title: Subtle RBI Rule Tweak Opens Door to $50 Billion Inflow to India
summary: A tweak to a single sentence within an arcane central bank circular has opened the door for billions of additional dollars to flow into India under a new incentive plan.

title: Apollo’s Kleinman Sees Private Equity Capitulating on Values
summary: Private equity “lost its way a little bit” during the easy-money era and firms will “have to start capitulating for sure on valuations” after borrowing costs normalized, warned Scott Kleinman, co-president of Apollo Global Management Inc.

title: Here’s a Look at How India Could Further Boost Dollar Inflows
summary: India is weighing further steps to attract foreign investment into the country, just days after rolling out a sweeping package aimed at boosting inflows and shoring up its external finances.

title: India Boosts LNG Buying to Meet Fertilizer, Power Requirements
summary: India has ramped up purchases of liquefied natural gas from the spot market as importers rush to replace disrupted Middle Eastern supply and meet demand from fertilizer plants, power generators and households.

title: Banks Pay 7% on Dollar Deposits as India Seeks Fresh Foreign Currency
summary: Indian lenders have sharply raised rates on foreign-currency deposits to lure the nation’s overseas residents, offering an early test of the central bank’s recent measures to bring in capital flows to support the rupee.

title: Hedge Funds on Asia Alpha
summary: Sam Kellie-Smith, CEO & Chief Investment Officer, APAC, Jain Global and Kenny Lam, CEO, Two Sigma Asia Pacific discuss how both liquidity, and opportunities, have grown in Asia with Bloomberg’s Erik Schatzker at Bloomberg Invest 2026 in Hong Kong.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Edaa on Market Infrastructure and Capital Flows
summary: Hanan Mohammad AlShehri, CEO, Securities Depository Center Company (Edaa), Saudi Tadawul Group discusses opening Saudi Arabia’s capital markets to foreign investors with Bloomberg’s Lulu Chen at Bloomberg Invest 2026 in Hong Kong.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Nippon Steel Gets ¥90 Billion Via First Bond Since US Steel
summary: Nippon Steel Corp. raised ¥90 billion ($560 million) via its first straight-bond offering since completing the acquisition of United States Steel Corp.

title: In Conversation With Clara Chan
summary: Clara Chan, CEO, Hong Kong Investment Corporation Ltd. discusses strategic early investment in AI and large language models with Bloomberg’s Stephen Engle at Bloomberg Invest 2026 in Hong Kong.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Korea Stock Hedging Surges to Level That Warned of Past Selloffs
summary: The number of bearish options tied to South Korea’s Kospi 200 Index has climbed so sharply relative to bullish wagers that it is nearing a level that previously foreshadowed market declines.

title: Bonnie Chan on IPO Momentum
summary: Bonnie Chan, CEO, Hong Kong Exchanges & Clearing Ltd. discusses Hong Kong’s IPO pipeline explaining issuers and investors a lot more engaged than in previous years. She was speaking to Bloomberg’s David Ingles at Bloomberg Invest 2026 in Hong Kong.  (Source: Bloomberg)
